spec_match.rationale: Touches core terms from the chromestatus summary but skips privacy-preserving, migrate-to-ua-ch — secondary spec surfaces are not exercised.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button, textarea); controls include "Reset to live"; "Get high-entropy values" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: One failure-mode signal is present but most edge cases (errors, unsupported environment, empty/zero inputs) are not demonstrated.

references.rationale: see-also section links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6224706757853184 so the cleanup script can recover this folder.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Surface the missing API surfaces on ua-field-annotator
openQuestions.detail: The chromestatus summary for Reduced User-Agent strings by default introduces privacy-preserving, migrate-to-ua-ch but the page does not mention or exercise them. Walk the spec and add a short labelled control or read-out for each, so the concept covers the headline API in full.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on ua-field-annotator
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

summary: Solid concept page for Reduced User-Agent strings by default: interactive, design-system clean, and references the chromestatus entry. Tighten edge-case coverage and the spec-vocabulary surface area to take it from good to canonical.
